one_line_thesis: Michigan mineral royalty optionality (>1M acres, Copperwood/Highland Copper leases) with patient balance sheet — asymmetric upside if mining develops, steady burn and single-tenant concentration until royalties activate.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Keweenaw Land is a Michigan mineral estate with a Copperwood free option on top of modest lease income. We expect -7.48% per year at today's price. FY2024 GAAP book is ~$12.70 per share; economic floor about $21 per share, not GAAP book. Component proof sum base ~$35.75 per share vs price ~$59.33. Management expects cash-flow positive from 2026 at the 2025 annual shareholder meeting [Management statement] (unverified vs H1 2025 net loss). Watch: price still embeds much of the option.

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
applicability_tests: The security owns a legally enforceable economic claim on scarce assets or royalties.
Material value is omitted or poorly represented by current GAAP earnings.
Asset quantities, ownership, overlap, timing, and remaining capital can be reconciled.
Comparable values can be adjusted for location, quality, control, liquidity, and realization time.
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.label: Existing lease and mineral-rights cash flows
components.cross_check: Reconcile the range to primary filings, a reproducible owner-cash or NAV bridge, and the identified liability and capital claims before decision use.

components.label: Copper, land, and water-development options
components.cross_check: Reconcile the range to primary filings, a reproducible owner-cash or NAV bridge, and the identified liability and capital claims before decision use.

components.label: Cash and other net claims
components.cross_check: Reconcile the range to primary filings, a reproducible owner-cash or NAV bridge, and the identified liability and capital claims before decision use.

components.label: Title, realization, and development-capital reserve
components.cross_check: Reconcile the range to primary filings, a reproducible owner-cash or NAV bridge, and the identified liability and capital claims before decision use.

economic_value.accounting_reference: Existing research and filing-derived valuation inputs. The primary-source reconciliation remains an open evidence gate.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of KEWL, including every currently identified operating claim, financial claim, liability, reserve, and material option.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and asset claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are separate components or explicitly reserved. No component is intended to overlap another.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Existing lease and mineral-rights cash flows
valuation_proof.evidence: FY2025 annual: 1,126,284 shares; >1.3M mineral acres; 36,705 leased acres. FY2024: book ~$14.3M (~$12.70/sh); mineral lease income ~$349.7K; ~$160K/yr operating burn. H1 2025 lease income $189K vs $149K prior year. Proof: 27.5/sh base via owner_cash_or_dividend_discount@1.0.
valuation_proof.adjustment: The low/base/high range incorporates ownership, tax, capital, cycle, timing, and realization uncertainty pending the primary bridge.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key current_operating_claim; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence shows that the economic claim, cash conversion, ownership, or required capital is materially worse than this low case.

valuation_proof.evidence: FY2025 annual: 1,126,284 shares; >1.3M mineral acres; 36,705 leased acres. FY2024: book ~$14.3M (~$12.70/sh); mineral lease income ~$349.7K; ~$160K/yr operating burn. H1 2025 lease income $189K vs $149K prior year. Proof: 9.9/sh base via probability_weighted_catalyst_nav@1.0.
valuation_proof.adjustment: The low/base/high range incorporates ownership, tax, capital, cycle, timing, and realization uncertainty pending the primary bridge.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: The range includes delay and failure outcomes; dated cash realization remains [HUMAN REVIEW].
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: Starter probability is explicit analyst judgment pending a primary-source milestone and ownership reconciliation.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Remaining owner-funded capital, financing, and dilution are reserved until the primary sources-and-uses bridge is complete.

bullets.title: Focused Compounding Clients · added · KEWL · 2019Q3
bullets.summary: Portfolio Overview: During the quarter, we completely sold off two stocks: Keweenaw Land Association (KEWL) and Computer Services (CSVI).

timeline.label: FY2025 OTC annual filed: >1.3M mineral acres, 36,705 leased acres, 1,126,284 shares outstanding
H1 2025 semi-annual: mineral lease income $189K vs $149K prior year; net loss ($51K)
FY2024: book ~$14.3M (~$12.70/sh); mineral lease income ~$349.7K; ~$160K/yr operating burn
Repurchased 51,633 shares at $27/sh; acquired 667,000 additional mineral acres (~$1.52/acre)
Timberland partial liquidation: ~$100/sh special distributions ($92 + $8) from timber asset sale; retained mineral business
Shareholder approval of timber sale and partial liquidation; company refocused on mineral leasing and royalties

industry.structure: KEWL is a pure mineral lessor on Michigan/Wisconsin acreage, not an operator. Revenue is lease rent; Copperwood royalties would accrue if Highland Copper reaches production. OTC Pink listing with thin liquidity and concentrated insider ownership (Mai ~29.6%, Speron ~11.6%).
industry.share_shift: After the 2021 timber liquidation the company shrank to a mineral-only estate. Highland Copper holds the dominant Copperwood lease; secondary lessees on the 667K-acre 2024 expansion remain the main incremental option.
industry.trend: Copper demand tailwind from electrification and data-center build-out supports option value, but spot price already embeds much of Copperwood upside at ~4.3× GAAP book. Michigan mining infrastructure grants could accelerate Highland Copper timing.

top_claims.claim: Keweenaw Land is a Michigan mineral estate with a Copperwood free option on top of modest lease income. We expect -8.29% per year (total synthesis at today's price). FY2024 GAAP book is ~$12.70 per share; economic floor about $21 per share, not GAAP book. Spot copper ~$6.67/lb (2026-06-02) scales the SSI royalty bridge
*Primary risk: Copperwood never reaches production while the stock trades at a large premium to book, forcing multiple compression toward ~$12.70.
Environmental / political block on Michigan mining.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Copperwood never reaches production while the stock trades at a large premium to book, forcing multiple compression toward ~$12.70.
Environmental / political block on Michigan mining.
Burn exceeds treasury before royalty cash flows.
